title: microstylis myurus
intro: Microstylis myurus, also known as the Indian squill or Himalayan squill, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its rhizomes and roots containing compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Microstylis myurus include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to aid in reducing fever and promoting digestion. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and powder, which are used to treat various ailments.
